understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with a '+' in the semantic grid, with all goals occupied at the same time before the step budget runs out.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is an active/selected piece that responds to actions. Using CLICK appears to move the selected piece in the direction of the clicked location, typically sliding along a row/column until it hits an obstacle/boundary and stops (e.g., CLICK on the left side moved it from [18, 21] to [9, 21], and CLICK on the right side moved it back). CLICKs that are not aligned with a valid travel direction, or where the path is blocked, cause no movement. Some attempted CLICKs in other areas also had no effect, suggesting either no piece could be selected there or the click did not define a legal move. Movement is constrained by walls/obstacles, and every action consumes 1 step from a limited per-level budget.

orient_history: Movement is grid-based in 1-cell (3px) steps and blocked by collidable walls. Since movement is fully blocked, the primary interaction may be CLICK-to-relocate/select/place a piece onto goal tiles despite wall enclosure.

CLICK likely changes selection when clicking a selectable; otherwise it may attempt to move/place the selected piece at the clicked location, but movement/placement can be blocked by walls/invalid paths. Since current piece is fully blocked, we should switch selection to test if the other selectable can move/place differently.

CLICK likely attempts to move the currently selected piece toward the clicked location (possibly snapping to grid / sliding until blocked). Need to get both selectables onto the goal area (any of the + cells) to trigger WIN.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves by 3 pixels (1 cell) but is blocked by collidable walls; CLICK may interact with objects (possibly swapping with another selectable). Since both pieces are fully enclosed, we need to test whether CLICK can also affect walls (e.g., toggle/remove) to create a path toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable changes the selected piece; CLICK on an empty/goal area may attempt to reposition the selected piece (possibly sliding/teleporting until blocked). Since MOVE is fully blocked, progress likely requires selecting the other piece and using CLICK-based movement.

CLICK on another selectable swaps the selected piece with the clicked selectable (as seen earlier when clicking near [19,22] swapped positions). To make progress, we likely need to select/swap to the other piece at [18,21] and see if it has any unblocked moves toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a piece when clicking on it; CLICK on other tiles may attempt to move/teleport the currently selected piece to that tile, but it might require clicking within the exact goal cell (center-aligned to 3px grid). Movement keys are irrelevant here due to enclosure.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable changes which piece is selected (the prior logs’ 'moved' messages were actually selection switching). Movement likely works only via MOVE_* if adjacent cell is empty; currently selected is trapped, so switch to the other piece to test its mobility/possible interactions.

Hypothesis: CLICK on the other selectable transfers (teleports/swaps) the currently-selected piece to that selectable’s position; this may be required before a CLICK on the goal can place a piece there.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable changes which piece is selected; movement with arrows is blocked in these pockets. To get to the goal, we may need a two-step interaction (select a piece first, then CLICK goal), so I will re-test by selecting the other piece as setup.

Clicks on a selectable likely change which piece is selected (the log text saying the 'selected piece moved' matches selection switching). To make progress we need to select the other piece and check if it has any available moves toward the goal region.

Hypothesis: CLICK can place/teleport the currently selected piece to certain valid tiles (likely the goal object), but the exact pixel target may matter; earlier goal clicks may have missed the actionable hotspot. MOVE_* is ineffective due to full surrounding walls.

Hypothesis: CLICK changes which selectable is active (it appears as the 'selected piece moved' in the log, but it's effectively selecting the piece at the clicked position). MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) and is prevented by adjacent walls. To progress, we should select the other piece and re-check if it has any available movement corridors toward the goal region.

CLICKing on a selectable changes the active/selected piece (the 'player') rather than moving it. MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 px) but can be blocked by walls. Next step: select the other piece to check if it has any available moves toward the goal.

CLICK seems to act as a direct interaction/teleport/selection mechanic keyed to exact pixel targets; movement keys are useless here because all adjacent tiles are walls. It may require clicking the goal’s exact origin pixel (top-left) rather than an interior point.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches control to that piece (engine reports this as the 'selected piece moved'). MOVE_* attempts to move the selected piece by 1 cell (3 px) but collision with walls prevents it. Possible missing mechanic: CLICKing on a wall tile might toggle/remove it (dig/open a path), since otherwise both pieces are fully enclosed and cannot reach the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable changes the active/selected piece (the engine reports this as 'selected piece moved'). Movement commands then attempt to move the currently selected piece; we need to find which selectable (if any) has a path to the goal region.

Hypothesis: CLICK may move/place the selected piece to the clicked cell if it is a valid goal/target tile; previous goal clicks may have missed the exact goal cell alignment. If this works, we can place both selectables onto distinct goal cells by selecting each and CLICKing goal cells.

CLICK likely attempts to move/place the currently selected 3x3 piece at the clicked pixel-aligned grid position (multiples of 3). Prior goal clicks may have missed the exact valid top-left grid coordinate for a goal cell, causing a blocked/no-effect result.

Hypothesis: CLICK acts on the tile/object under the cursor; selecting/activating pieces works reliably, but placing onto the goal may require clicking a specific point inside the goal (likely its center) rather than its edge. Movement by arrows is impossible due to the pieces being enclosed by walls, so winning likely requires CLICK-based placement onto the goal area.

CLICK appears to switch which selectable is active (the log reports the 'selected piece moved', but it likely indicates selection changed to the other piece). To make progress we must select the other piece and re-check its movement options.

CLICKing on a selectable likely switches which piece is selected (the log reports it as the selected piece 'moving' because the controlled entity changes). Arrow-key movement depends on local walls, so the other piece may have different available moves.

Hypothesis: CLICK can change which selectable is active by clicking in/near its cell (and may also move/swap). Since the selected piece at [9,21] is immobile, we likely must switch control to the other selectable at [18,21] to find a position with an open move route toward the goal.

Hypothesis: Movement keys move by one cell but walls fully enclose each selectable. CLICK on a selectable switches the active/selected piece; other CLICKs (e.g., on the goal) have had no effect so far, but it may depend on which piece is selected.

Working hypothesis: CLICK can potentially toggle/remove a wall tile if targeted exactly on a wall cell; otherwise it either changes selection (when clicking the other selectable) or has no effect. MOVE is standard grid movement but currently blocked by adjacent walls.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* only works when adjacent cell is free (currently all blocked). CLICK on another selectable swaps the selected piece into that selectable's position (and likely swaps the other back), enabling limited repositioning/selection changes; clicks on walls/goals/empty have had no effect so far.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects/interacts with objects/tiles; clicks on another selectable switch selection, while clicks on certain empty/goal tiles may teleport the currently selected piece if the coordinate hits the valid target region. Movement keys are irrelevant because pieces are enclosed by walls.

Hypothesis: CLICK either selects a piece when clicking on it, or attempts to place/teleport the currently selected piece onto the clicked goal tile if the click is on the goal’s true anchor pixel (may require exact [35,17]). Since MOVE is fully blocked, winning likely requires a precise CLICK onto the goal tile.

CLICK appears to act as a special relocation/selection mechanic (possibly teleporting the controllable piece to certain valid target tiles such as other selectable anchors). MOVE is currently impossible due to walls, so the only remaining chance is that CLICKing directly on a goal tile (at the correct cell/pixel) may teleport/place the piece onto it if the click lands on an exact valid goal cell.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is currently controlled (earlier 'moved' results were actually selection swapping between the two identical pieces). Direct movement is by MOVE_* one cell (3 px), but the currently selected piece is boxed in. To have any chance, we should select the other piece and see if it has any available moves.

Hypothesis: CLICK moves/teleports the selected piece only when clicking on a valid destination/anchor (likely another selectable/teleport pad). Since movement is fully walled-in, the only remaining chance is that a precise click inside the goal area triggers a move/placement mechanic that earlier clicks missed due to position.

Clicks can teleport/swap the selected piece between certain valid locations; movement is otherwise blocked by walls. With 0 steps left, normal progression is likely impossible, but a CLICK might still be processed (or might fail due to no steps).

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with a '+' in the semantic grid, with all goals occupied at the same time before the step budget runs out.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is an active/selected piece that responds to actions. Using CLICK appears to move the selected piece in the direction of the clicked location, typically sliding along a row/column until it hits an obstacle/boundary and stops (e.g., CLICK on the left side moved it from [18, 21] to [9, 21], and CLICK on the right side moved it back). CLICKs that are not aligned with a valid travel direction, or where the path is blocked, cause no movement. Some attempted CLICKs in other areas also had no effect, suggesting either no piece could be selected there or the click did not define a legal move. Movement is constrained by walls/obstacles, and every action consumes 1 step from a limited per-level budget.
